name: Ranpak Holdings Corp.
business: Ranpak Holdings Corp. is a leading provider of Protective Packaging Solutions products and end-of-line automation solutions for e-commerce and industrial supply chains. The company sells paper-based packaging consumables and automated equipment through a network of over 250 distributors and directly to select end-users.
sellsTo: industrial manufacturing end-users (approximately 11% of net revenue)
industrial machinery end-users (approximately 6% of net revenue)
other end-users including automotive aftermarket, electronics, pharmaceuticals, home furnishing, food and beverage, and warehousing (approximately 43% of net revenue in aggregate)

exposures.sentence: Approximately 40% of our net revenue is derived from sales to e-commerce end-users.
Our Automation products represented 10% of our net revenue in 2025, compared to 8% of our net revenue in 2024.

In 2021, we established R Squared Robotics, a division of Ranpak, that uses three-dimensional computer vision and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to enhance end-of-line packaging and logistics.
Pickle is a robotics-solutions company which has developed robots for sorting, loading and unloading packaged goods.

Our Automation and Machine Vision product lines provide many high-volume businesses with significant improvements to end-of-line packaging speed, reduced labor, material and shipping costs, and the generation of valuable data and statistics on end-of-line performance.

We have expanded our offering to include fiber-based liners and sustainable plant-based cool packs to keep perishable goods cool while they are being delivered to consumers, such as the RecyCold climaliner Plus.

catalysts.event: Malaysia paper conversion facility became operational
catalysts.sentence: Our full-service paper conversion facility in Malaysia became operational in the second half of 2024, and we believe the Malaysia facility has continued to improve our ability to serve customers in the region by shortening lead times as well and providing a more attractive cost profile to the Asia Pacific (APAC) market than we have historically been able to offer.
